Read provides AI generated meeting summaries to make meetings more effective and efficient. View our Privacy Policy at https://www.read.ai/pp

Type "read stop" to disable, or "opt out" to delete meeting data.

00:50:25	Potuz:	it would be very nice to bench less than 3 seconds into the slot for attestations, I'd love if we could go to at least 2500ms and then reduce the deadline for Bid calling from 1 second to 300ms or similar
00:51:10	Nico Flaig:	Reacted to "it would be very nic..." with 👍
00:51:11	Justin Traglia:	Replying to "@Justin Traglia it s..."

Yes,

Attestation deadline = 3 seconds
Payload deadline = 6 seconds
PTC attestation = 9 seconds
00:51:11	Barnabas:	Replying to "it would be very nic..."

would expect that we would need to have mainnet with epbs enabled before we could evaluate this. I’d propose such changes for hegota.
00:51:27	Potuz:	Replying to "it would be very nic..."

we do not need a hard fork for this
00:52:03	Marius Van Der Wijden (M):	Replying to "To be precise 3 also..." 

 If that would be easy, we would've done it already, the problem is that you can't parallelize building and now with bals it becomes even harder
00:52:09	Potuz:	Replying to "it would be very nic..."

if clients ship with lowering the default deadlinde for bid selection and after a release we release with the 2500ms attestation, there shouldn't be splits
00:52:38	Dustin:	Replying to "it would be very n..."

has this been measured with some trickier regions of the world?
00:52:41	Potuz:	Replying to "To be precise 3 also..."

@Marius Van Der Wijden (M) the 3 seconds is irrelevant for local builders to build a decent payload
00:52:48	Potuz:	Replying to "To be precise 3 also..."

the payload is built way in advance
00:52:55	Potuz:	Replying to "To be precise 3 also..."

in fact the lower the deadline is better for local builders
00:53:09	Ansgar Dietrichs:	now is it a privacy dapp sender, or a privacyd app sender
00:53:17	Dustin:	Replying to "it would be very n..."

devnets don't count, they're all datacenter nodes
00:53:26	Potuz:	Replying to "To be precise 3 also..."

Carlos' point on slot 0 for local builders I believe is gone with ePBS that local builders don't need to broadcast blobs in 2 seconds
00:53:43	Potuz:	Replying to "To be precise 3 also..."

and just for clarity: the payload is built before the slot even starts
00:53:59	Nico Flaig:	Replying to "it would be very nic..."

I do think this should be changed only at a hard fork, users are slow to upgrade outside of it
00:54:22	Potuz:	Replying to "it would be very nic..."

yes, we've had previously tested geographically separated nodes with very large blocks, and ePBS will have very small blocks
00:55:04	Potuz:	Replying to "it would be very nic..."

@Nico Flaig users delaying the upgrade also does not lead to split views
00:55:14	Toni Wahrstätter:	Replying to "To be precise 3 also helps local builders to build a decebt payload "

agree with potuz but would say that we should not assume that the payload is built before the slot starts. With epbs, having the full slot for execution, we scale such that weak nodes finish execution just before the next slot (which is the earliest they can start building if they don't build optimistically using/trusting the BAL)
00:55:49	Toni Wahrstätter:	Replying to "To be precise 3 also helps local builders to build a decebt payload "

the slowest (executing) nodes shouldn't build locally if they want to build decent blocks
00:56:12	Potuz:	Replying to "To be precise 3 also..."

that is really the total edge case of a local builder that won't take a bid and that is weak enough that just finished executing at second 0
00:56:51	Potuz:	Replying to "To be precise 3 also..."

giving out a full second of timing games for every single block just for this case seems crazy.
00:57:50	Toni Wahrstätter:	Replying to "To be precise 3 also helps local builders to build a decebt payload "

agree but the delta between slowest and fastest nodes will always end up allowing timing games
00:58:12	Potuz:	Replying to "To be precise 3 also..."

yeah that one I give to you.
00:58:52	Toni Wahrstätter:	Also, shipping those two EIPs together (on top of frame) is nice for dapps as they won't need to redesign they contract another time but only once.
00:59:11	Iván | ethrex:	Reacted to "Also, shipping those..." with 🔥
00:59:28	CPerezz:	Replying to "To be precise 3 also..."

@Marius Van Der Wijden (M) Geth improved drastically when with Bloatnet we realized block production performance was terrible.

So not really sure I agree with your premise
00:59:42	soispoke:	A réagi à "Also, shipping tho..." avec 🔥
01:00:04	Nico Flaig:	Replying to "it would be very nic..."

but a node that didn't update votes differently, so you lose the head vote reward, the bid deadline is not relevant if operators run modified clients to play timing games
01:00:07	CPerezz:	Replying to "To be precise 3 also..."

Though true the more work we put on the prover, the harder it is.. that is undeniable
01:00:20	soispoke:	link to today's presentation: https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/12HAIc8F0u5Rt3XGG9l_C-JK8h_BuSoERvOLXIGPLu9c/edit?usp=sharing
 
link to 8250: https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-8250
link to 8272: https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-8272
01:00:20	lightclient:	it’s not for the block root
01:00:30	soispoke:	it's for the privacy protocol merkle root
01:01:03	ignacio:	Unless there is a standard about that merkle root being in a particular storage slot in these kind of contracts, I think you can't link them in an MPT yea
01:01:18	frangio:	there is a roots system contract
01:01:34	Ansgar Dietrichs:	is it not about VOPS?
01:01:41	Felix (Geth):	I think what Guillaume is asking is, why can't we prove against a block root
01:02:05	Francesco Risitano (Tau):	Why not just have an MMR over block roots
01:02:07	ignacio:	Replying to "Unless there is a st..."

Maybe not a standard but clearly defined per contract
01:02:19	CPerezz:	Reacted to "Why not just have an..." with 👀
01:02:27	Toni Wahrstätter:	https://nerolation.github.io/hegota-eip-presentations/hegota-pfi-proposal.html#1
01:04:29	Dustin:	why is this something to cater to? zk yes, sure, but specifically try building 10k or whatever blocks
01:04:54	Justin Florentine (Besu):	Replying to "why is this somethin..."

it's a ton less hashing
01:05:19	Dustin:	the usecase as pitched is, make MEV builders' jobs easier
01:05:20	Dustin:	I mean.
01:07:25	Dragan Rakita:	Replying to "the usecase as pitch..."

What is bottlenecks on reducing block times from 12s to something like 6s or 3s?
01:08:49	Jihoon:	Just a nit, it will be newPayloadV6
01:09:44	Kamil Salakhiev:	if these sidecars are large, might make sense to consider EC broadcast instead of gossipsub (assuming EC broadcast also lands on Hegota)
01:10:35	Kamil Salakhiev:	Replying to "if these sidecars ar..."

1MB is really large message for gossipsub
01:10:37	ignacio:	Provers can separate the provingo of post-state root of previous block, from subsequent block execution. Still unsure what this means reg potentially having to aggregate the proof. But worth creating some doc were we can explore this in more detail and we can share tradeoffs.
01:11:36	Ben Adams:	https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-8298
01:11:36	Karim T. (matkt):	If we calculate the state root of the previous block, we can start without waiting the BAL of the new block it seems @Toni Wahrstätter  , so do that between two blocks
01:12:28	Toni Wahrstätter:	Replying to "the usecase as pitched is, make MEV builders' jobs easier"

builders are also part of the ecosystem and simplifying things for them also reduces entry barriers for new builders
01:13:00	Toni Wahrstätter:	Replying to "the usecase as pitched is, make MEV builders' jobs easier"

The big builders care less about delaying the state root bc they invested a lot of time and resources in optimizing that. New builders struggle catching up.
01:13:23	CPerezz:	Propagation? We can div by 2 the gas limit and the block time. And do it as much as we want. What we don’t get a linear reduction on might be on bandwidth capacity and propagation speed. @Dragan Rakita
01:13:42	Toni Wahrstätter:	Replying to "If we calculate the state root of the previous block, we can start without waiting the BAL of the new block it seems @Toni Wahrstätter  , so do that between two blocks"

right yeah, you could use the BAL of the prev slot. This means, also block builders can now profit from it
01:14:08	Justin Florentine (Besu):	Replying to "the usecase as pitch..."

the thing with optimizing for building is the value yielded only ever accrues back to the builder
01:15:14	Luis Pinto | Besu:	Seems interesting at a glance
01:15:28	Toni Wahrstätter:	Replying to "the usecase as pitched is, make MEV builders' jobs easier"

Are you sure? One could also argue that making building simpler, creating a more leveled playingfield, allows for more smaller builders that are less sophisticated to enter, thus making things more competetitve and being beneficial for proposers
01:16:33	Karim T. (matkt):	Reacted to "right yeah, you coul..." with 👍
01:16:42	Barnabas:	which one?
01:17:02	Pooja Ranjan:	https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-7610
01:17:43	Barnabas:	Will add it on our list
01:17:43	Justin Florentine (Besu):	Replying to "the usecase as pitch..."

i'm mostly sure, but just mostly. in order for that value to manifest as a more diverse market of buidlers, it would have to help smaller builders more than established builders, otherwise its a wash. seems like consolidation is only affected evenly here.
